title: Infoshare changes by date | Stats NZ
content:
## Infoshare changes by date
Ann ouncements about revisions, corrections, and new series in Infoshare.
See also Infoshare changes by date – older changes .

### 9 June 2026: Business financial data (BDC) and Manufacturing (MFG)
We received updated information and made improvements to our methodology that affect data from the March 2023 to the December 2025 quarters. The changes are marked with a revision flag on the affected series in the published tables, CSV files, and Infoshare series.
The impacted series are:
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)CC1CA – Food, beverage, and tobacco product manufacturing – sales, purchases, salaries and wages, operating profit – the corresponding higher-level industries were not impacted by this change
- BDCQ.SF(3,8)CC3CA – Wood and paper products manufacturing – salaries and wages, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(3,8)CCCA
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)CC9CA – Furniture and other manufacturing – sales, purchases, salaries and wages, operating profit – the corresponding higher-level industries were not impacted by this change
- BDCQ.SF(2,8)FF1CA – Wholesale trade – purchases, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(2,8)FFCA
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)II1CA – Transport, postal, and warehousing – sales, purchases, salaries and wages, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)IICA
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)JJ1CA – Information media and telecommunications – sales, purchases, salaries and wages, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)JJCA
- BDCQ.SF(3,8)LL1CA – Rental, hiring, and real estate services – salaries and wages, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(3,8)LLCA
- MFGQ. SFA(4,5)(C,K)A – Meat and dairy product manufacturing – raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – current prices and deflated – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SFZ(4,5)(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFD(1,2,4,5)(C,K)A – Beverage and tobacco product manufacturing – sales, purchases, raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – current prices and deflated – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(1,2,4,5)(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFL(4,5)(C,K)A – Transport equipment, machinery and equipment manufacturing – raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – current prices and deflated – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(4,5)(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFM(1,2,4,5)(C,K)A – Furniture and other manufacturing – sales, purchases, raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – current prices and deflated – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(1,2,4,5)(C,K)A
Subject category: Industry sectors

### 4 June 2026: Livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS)
We have revised the livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS) covering the period April 2025 to April 2026. The month revised was in March 2026. LSS regions that were revised: Regional Council by kill animal type – LSSM.SAA – Northland, Auckland, Waikato, Bay of Plenty Total New Zealand by kill by animal type – LSSM.SAZ Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Livestock slaughtering statistics

### 22 May 2026: Retail trade survey (RTS)
We have made revisions that affect data in the December 2025 quarter.
These changes are marked with a revision flag on the affected series in the published tables, CSV files, and relevant Infoshare series.
The impacted series are:
- RTTQ.SFQ9CA – Fuel retailing, total stocks
- RTTQ.SF99CA – All industries total, total stocks
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Retail Trade (ANZSIC06) – RTT

### 7 May 2026: Livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS)
We have revised the livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS) covering the period March 2025 to March 2026. The month revised was in February 2026. LSS regions that were revised: Regional Council by kill animal type – LSSM.SAC – Taranaki, Wanganui, Manawatu, Wellington Total New Zealand by kill by animal type – LSSM.SAZ Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Livestock slaughtering statistics

### 1 May 2026: Building consents issued (BLD)
Building consents issued: March 2026 included a routine update to geographies. All subnational series have been updated to use 2026 geographic areas.
Series where cells were changed have been marked R for revised. Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Building consents – BLD

### 23 April 2026: Research and development survey (RAD)
We have updated the 2023 and 2024 figures due to updated respondent data. Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Research and development survey – RAD

### 16 April 2026: Agricultural production statistics (AGR)
Nelson region statistics for 2025 are suppressed because the sampling error could not be calculated for this region.
See the technical notes for more information.
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Agriculture

### 7 April 2026: Dwelling and household estimates (DDE)
We have revised the private dwelling and household estimates from March 2018 to December 2025. This revision is to correct an error in the base (starting point) derived from the 2023 Census, where dwellings under construction were incorrectly included in the estimates. The revision also includes a correction to the tenure ratios derived from the 2023 Census.
We apologise for any inconvenience this may have caused.
The affected series are:
- DDEQ.SH(B,C,D) – Estimated households in private occupied dwellings, as at quarter ended
- DDEQ.SI(B,C,D) – Estimated households in private occupied dwellings, mean quarter ended
- DDEQ.SJ(B,C,D) – Estimated households in private occupied dwellings, mean year ended
- DDEQ.SK(A,B,C,D) – Estimated private dwellings, as at quarter ended
- DDEQ.SL(A,B,C,D) – Estimated private dwellings, mean quarter ended
- DDEQ.SM(A,B,C,D) – Estimated private dwellings, mean year ended.
Dwelling and household estimates: March 2026 quarter has more information.
Subject category: Population Group: Demography Dwelling and Household Estimates – DDE

### 2 April 2026: Livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS)
We have revised the livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS) covering the period February 2025 to February 2026. The period that was revised covered September 2025 through January 2026.
LSS regions that were revised:
Regional Council by kill animal type – LSSM.SAA – Northland, Auckland, Waikato, Bay of Plenty
Total New Zealand by kill by animal type – LSSM.SAZ Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Livestock slaughtering statistics

### 25 March 2026: International travel and migration (ITM)
We updated the series 'Estimated migrant arrivals to Australia by country of last permanent residence (CLPR), citizenship, and birthplace'.
The update includes finalised estimates for the September 2024 quarter, revised provisional estimates for December 2024 to June 2025 quarters, and provisional estimates for the September 2025 quarter.
Series identifier: ITMQ.SEMG+++Z++Z++Z Subject category: Tourism

### 17 March 2026: Selected price indexes (SPI)
We have corrected the SPI Food group (CPIM.SE901) and SPI Alcoholic beverages and tobacco group (CPIM.SE902) for January 2026. This has included corrections for subitems, items, and classes in the following subgroup level indexes:
- CPIM.SE9011 Fruit and vegetables
- CPIM.SE9012 Meat, poultry, and fish
- CPIM.SE9013 Grocery food
- CPIM.SE9014 Non-alcoholic beverages
- CPIM.SE9015 Restaurant meal and ready-to-eat food
- CPIM.SE9021 Alcoholic beverages
- CPIM.SE9022 Cigarettes and tobacco.
Meat, poultry, and fish, grocery food, non-alcoholic beverages, alcoholic beverages, and cigarettes and tobacco have also been corrected at subgroup level.
For these series, we have corrected:
- Index numbers and percentage change from the: previous month, and same month of the previous year.
- same month of the previous year.
- Weighted average retail prices of selected items, and percentage change from the: previous month, and same month of the previous year.
- same month of the previous year.
- Distribution of national item-level index movements from the previous month.
Subject category: Economic indicators Group: Consumers price index – CPI

### 12 March 2026: Business financial data (BDC), Manufacturing (MFG), and Wholesale trade (WTS)
We received updated information and made improvements to our methodology that affect data from the September 2024 to September 2025 quarters.
These changes are marked with a revision flag on the affected series in the published tables, CSV files, and relevant Infoshare series.
The impacted series are:
- BDCQ. SF(1,2,3,8)CC1CA – Food, beverage, and tobacco product manufacturing – sales, purchases, salaries and wages, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)CCCA
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,8)FF1CA – Wholesale trade –sales, purchases, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries –BDCQ.SF(1,2,8)FFCA
- BDCQ.SF(2,8)JJ1CA – Information media and telecommunications – purchases, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(2,8)JJCA
- BDCQ.SF(3,8)MN2CA – Administrative and support services – salaries and wages, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(3,8)MNCA
- MFGQ. SFA(1,2)(C,K)A – Meat and dairy product manufacturing – sales, purchases – current prices and deflated – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SFZ(1,2)(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFC4CA – Fruit, oil, cereal and other food manufacturing – raw materials stocks – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)4CA
- MFGQ.SFL5(C,K)A – Transport equipment, machinery and equipment manufacturing – finished goods stocks – current prices and deflated and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)5(C,K)A
- WTSQ.SFD(1,9)CA – Grocery, liquor and tobacco product wholesaling – sales, stocks – and the corresponding higher-level wholesaling industries – WTSQ.SFZ(1,9)CA
Subject category: Industry sectors

### 5 March 2026: Livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS)
We have revised the livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS) covering the period January 2025 to January 2026. The month revised was in December 2025.
LSS regions that were revised:
Regional Council by kill animal type – LSSM.SAC – Taranaki, Wanganui, Manawatu, Wellington
Total New Zealand by kill by animal type – LSSM.SAZ
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Livestock slaughtering statistics

### 3 March 2026: Tourism satellite account (TSA)
We have updated the tourism satellite account figures for the following three series after correcting an error:
- TSAA.S1AE(1,2,3,4,5,6,9)RM – Other tourism-related services
- TSAA.S1AE(1,2,3,4,5,6,9)RN – Other personal services
- TSAA.S1AE(1,2,3,4,5,6,9)RO – Education services.
Subject category: Tourism Group: Tourism satellite account – TSA

### 29 January 2026: Overseas merchandise trade (OMT)
Previously reported provisional quantities of logs (HS4403) exported to China for the month of November 2025 have been updated with the release of Overseas merchandise trade: December 2025 . Subject category: Imports and exports

### 14 January 2026: Monthly employment indicators
We have made minor updates to the Employment indicators: July 2025 figures.
The affected series are:
- MEIM.S1(2,4) – High level industry (All industries)
- MEIM.SA – Industry (All industries)
- MEIM.SB – Region (All regions)
- MEIM.SC – Age and region (All ages and regions)
- MEIM.SD – Sex and region (All sexes and regions)
- MEIM.SE – Sex and age (All sexes and ages)
- MEIQ.S2W(X,Y,Z)A1 – High level industry (All industries).
The changes are noted with a revision flag on the affected series in the CSV files and relevant Infoshare series.
Subject category: Work, income, and spending Group: Employment indicators – MEI

### 13 January 2026: Dwelling and household estimates (DDE)
We will revise the private dwelling and household estimates from March 2018 to December 2025, with the revised data to be released in Dwelling and household estimates: March 2026 quarter , scheduled for publication on 7 April 2026. This revision is to correct an error in the 2023 Census base, where dwellings under construction were incorrectly included.
The affected series are:
- DDEQ.SH(B,C,D) – Estimated households in private occupied dwellings, as at quarter ended
- DDEQ.SI(B,C,D) – Estimated households in private occupied dwellings, mean quarter ended
- DDEQ.SJ(B,C,D) – Estimated households in private occupied dwellings, mean year ended
- DDEQ.SK(A,B,C,D) – Estimated private dwellings, as at quarter ended
- DDEQ.SL(A,B,C,D) – Estimated private dwellings, mean quarter ended
- DDEQ.SM(A,B,C,D) – Estimated private dwellings, mean year ended.
Dwelling and household estimates: December 2025 quarter has more information.
Subject category: Population Group: Demography Dwelling and Household Estimates – DDE

### 23 December 2025: Overseas merchandise trade (OMT)
Provisional quantities of logs (HS4403) exported to China have been overstated for the month of November 2025. We've received amended values and the updated provisional figures will be released with Overseas merchandise trade: December 2025 , scheduled for release on 29 January 2026.
Subject category: Imports and exports

### 22 December 2025: International travel and migration (ITM)
We updated the series ‘Estimated migrant arrivals to Australia by country of last permanent residence (CLPR), citizenship, and birthplace’. The update includes finalised estimates for the June 2024 quarter, revised provisional estimates for September 2024 to March 2025 quarters, and provisional estimates for the June 2025 quarter. Series identifier: ITMQ.SEMG+++Z++Z++Z Subject category: Tourism

### 19 December 2025: Business employment data (BED)
Gross earnings data for Business employment data: September 2025 quarter was released on 19 December at 2pm, after a delay in publishing the data. Filled jobs data for the September 2025 quarter also includes very minor revisions.
The affected series are:
- BDCQ.SEA – Industry (All industries)
- BDCQ.SEB – Sex (All sexes)
- BDCQ.SEC – Age (All ages)
- BDCQ.SED – Region (All regions)
- BDCQ.SEE – Territorial authority (All territorial authorities).
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Business data collection – BDC

### 16 December 2025: Selected price indexes (SPI)
We have reinstated the annual and monthly price indexes for the flow of rental properties (national and regional) in the Selected price indexes: November 2025 release.
The following time series from December 2024 to November 2025 have been included:
- CPIM.SE9041F – New Zealand, flow of rental properties
- CPIM.SE1041F – Auckland, flow of rental properties
- CPIM.SE2041F – Wellington, flow of rental properties
- CPIM.SE3041F – Rest of North Island, flow of rental properties
- CPIM.SE5041F – Canterbury, flow of rental properties
- CPIM.SE6041F – Rest of South Island, flow of rental properties.
Some caution is advised about the quality of the data between December 2024 and May 2025.
Subject category: Economic indicators Group: Consumers price index – CPI

### 11 December 2025: Business financial data (BDC), Manufacturing (MFG)
We received updated information or made improvements to our methodology affecting the seven quarters from December 2023 to June 2025.
The changes are noted with a revision flag on the affected series in the published tables, CSV files, and relevant Infoshare series.
The impacted series are:
- BDCQ.SF(2,8)CC1CA – Food, beverage, and tobacco product manufacturing –purchases, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(2,8)CCCA
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)CC7CA – Metal product manufacturing – sales, purchases, salaries and wages, operating profit – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)CCCA
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,8)II1CA – Transport, postal and warehousing – sales, purchases, and operating profit
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)MN1CA – Professional, scientific, and technical services – sales, purchases, salaries and wages, operating profit – and the corresponding higher level industries – BDCQ.SF(1,2,3,8)MNCA – Professional, scientific, technical, administrative and support services
- MFGQ.SFC(4,5)(C,K)A – Fruit, oil, cereal, and other food manufacturing – raw materials, finished goods stocks – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(4,5)(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFD(2,4,5)(C,K)A – Beverage and tobacco manufacturing – purchases, raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(2,4,5)(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFF(4,5)(C,K)A – Wood and paper product manufacturing – raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(4,5)(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFI(4,5)(C,K)A – Chemical, polymer, and rubber product manufacturing – raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(4,5)(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFK(1,2,4,5)(C,K)A – Metal product manufacturing – sales, purchases, raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – and the corresponding higher-level manufacturing industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(1,2,4,5)(C,K)A.
Subject category: Industry sectors

### 11 December 2025: Business employment data (BED)
Gross earnings data for Business employment data: September 2025 quarter has not been released due to issues identified during data preparation. Gross earnings data will be published as soon as this is available.
The affected series are:
- BDCQ.SEA2 - Industry (All industries)
- BDCQ.SEB2 - Sex (All sexes)
- BDCQ.SEC2 - Age (All ages)
- BDCQ.SED2 - Region (All regions)
- BDCQ.SEE2 - Territorial authority (All territorial authorities)
Filled jobs data for the September 2025 quarter is available but may be subject to minor updates with the gross earnings update.
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Business data collection – BDC

### 5 December 2025: Livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS)
We have revised the livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS) covering the period October 2024 to October 2025. The month revised was in July 2025.
LSS regions that were revised:
Regional Council by kill animal type – LSSM.SAA – Northland, Auckland, Waikato, Bay of Plenty
Total New Zealand by kill by animal type – LSSM.SAZ
Subject category: Livestock slaughtering statistics Group: Industry sectors

### 2 December 2025: Retail trade survey (RTS)
We have corrected an error in the Retail trade survey: September 2025 release affecting revised stocks series from September 2022 to June 2025.
The corrected series are:
- RTTQ.SFA9CA – Supermarket and grocery stores
- RTTQ.SFG9CA – Furniture, floor coverings, houseware, textiles
- RTTQ.SFH9CA – Hardware, building, and garden supplies
- RTTQ.SFK9CA – Clothing, footwear, and accessories
- RTTQ.SFL9CA – Electrical and electronic goods
- RTTQ.SFP9CA – Motor vehicles and parts
- RTTQ.SF19CA – Core industries total
- RTTQ.SF99CA – All industries total
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Retail Trade (ANZSIC06) – RTT

### 27 November 2025: Retail trade survey (RTS)
We have made improvements to our stock methodology for some series affecting the September 2022 to June 2025 quarters.
The changes are noted with a revision flag on the affected series in the published tables, CSV files, and relevant Infoshare series.
The impacted stocks series are:
- RTTQ.SFA9CA – Supermarket and grocery stores
- RTTQ.SFG9CA – Furniture, floor coverings, houseware, textiles
- RTTQ.SFH9CA – Hardware, building, and garden supplies
- RTTQ.SFK9CA – Clothing, footwear, and accessories
- RTTQ.SFL9CA – Electrical and electronic goods
- RTTQ.SFP9CA – Motor vehicles and parts
- RTTQ.SF19CA – Core industries total
- RTTQ.SF99CA – All industries total
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Retail Trade (ANZSIC06) – RTT

### 14 November 2025: National accounts (income and expenditure): Year ended March 2024
We have updated some status flags in this data. TSM code is “SNEA.SG06NAC00D45” and the year affected is 2023 (March Year-end)
Subject category: Economic indicators Group: National accounts (for consolidated accounts) Group: Institutional sector accounts (for sector accounts data)

### 14 November 2025: National accounts (industry production and investment): Year ended March 2023
We have updated some status flags in this data. TSM code is “SNEA.SG06NAC00D45” and the year affected is 2023 (March Year-end)
Subject category: Economic indicators Group: National accounts

### 28 October 2025: Household living-costs price indexes (HLPI)
From the March 2025 quarter onwards, as part of the HLPI review changes, the following series have been discontinued. From the ‘HLPI Sub-Groups’ table:
- HPIQ.S(ALL,BEN,SUP,MAO,IQ1,IQ2,IQ3,IQ4,IQ5,EQ1,EQ2,EQ3,EQ4,EQ5)042 – Home ownership
From the ‘HLPI Non-standard series’ table:
- HPIQ.S(ALL,BEN,SUP,MAO,IQ1,IQ2,IQ3,IQ4,IQ5,EQ1,EQ2,EQ3,EQ4,EQ5)NS1120 – All groups less central and local government charges
- HPIQ.S(ALL,BEN,SUP,MAO,IQ1,IQ2,IQ3,IQ4,IQ5,EQ1,EQ2,EQ3,EQ4,EQ5)NS1410 – All groups less household energy subgroup and vehicle fuels
- HPIQ.S(ALL,BEN,SUP,MAO,IQ1,IQ2,IQ3,IQ4,IQ5,EQ1,EQ2,EQ3,EQ4,EQ5)NS1420 – All groups less vehicle fuels
- HPIQ.S(ALL,BEN,SUP,MAO,IQ1,IQ2,IQ3,IQ4,IQ5,EQ1,EQ2,EQ3,EQ4,EQ5)NS1430 – All groups less food group and vehicle fuels
- HPIQ.S(ALL,BEN,SUP,MAO,IQ1,IQ2,IQ3,IQ4,IQ5,EQ1,EQ2,EQ3,EQ4,EQ5)NS1450 – All groups less food groups, household energy subgroup and vehicle fuels
- HPIQ.S(ALL,BEN,SUP,MAO,IQ1,IQ2,IQ3,IQ4,IQ5,EQ1,EQ2,EQ3,EQ4,EQ5)NS1510 – All groups less food groups, household energy subgroup, vehicle fuels, and government charges
From the ‘HLPI Non-standard series 3’ table:
- HPIQ.S(ALL,BEN,SUP,MAO,IQ1,IQ2,IQ3,IQ4,IQ5,EQ1,EQ2,EQ3,EQ4,EQ5)NS1130 – All groups – goods component
- HPIQ.S(ALL,BEN,SUP,MAO,IQ1,IQ2,IQ3,IQ4,IQ5,EQ1,EQ2,EQ3,EQ4,EQ5)NS1140 – All groups – service component
Subject category: Economic indicators Group: Household living costs price indexes – HPI

### 21 October 2025: Transport vehicle registrations (TPT)
We have made updates to the September 2025 figures for the following 28 series after correcting an error. Series beginning:
The changes are noted with a revision flag on the affected series in the CSV files and relevant Infoshare series.
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Transport – TPT

### 16 October 2025: Selected price indexes (SPI)
We have corrected annual and monthly price index movements in the food price index (FPI) for September 2025.
Subject category: Economic indicators Group: Consumers price index – CPI

### 3 October 2025: Livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS)
We have revised the livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS) covering the period August 2024 to August 2025. The revised months are June 2025 and July 2025.
LSS regions that were revised:
Regional council by kill animal type – LSSM.SAE – Canterbury Regional council by kill animal type – LSSM.SAF – Otago, Southland
Total New Zealand by kill by animal type – LSSM.SAZ
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Livestock slaughtering statistics

### 3 October 2025: Balance of payments and international investment position: Year ended 31 March 2025
We have updated some status flags in this data.
Subject category: Economic indicators Group: Balance of payments – BOP Group: International investment position – IIP

### 24 September 2025: International travel and migration (ITM)
We updated the series ‘Estimated migrant arrivals to Australia by country of last permanent residence (CLPR), citizenship, and birthplace’. The update includes finalised estimates for the March 2024 quarter, revised provisional estimates for June 2024 to December 2024 quarters, and provisional estimates for the March 2025 quarter. Series identifier: ITMQ.SEMG+++Z++Z++Z
Subject category: Tourism

### 19 September 2025: Overseas merchandise trade (OMT)
Resolved 24 September 2025 The harmonised trade tables will not be available with Overseas merchandise trade: August 2025 , due to ongoing technical issues with Infoshare.

### 19 September 2025: Overseas merchandise trade (OMT)
We have corrected a processing error identified in our imports and exports data for the March 2025 month.
Subject category: Imports and exports

### 18 September 2025: Gross domestic product (GDP)
Resolved 22 September 2025 We are continuing to experience technical issues with Infoshare Browse that may mean data from Gross domestic product: June 2025 quarter will not be available in that view. All GDP data for the June 2025 quarter will be available at 10:45am via Infoshare Export Direct, as well as in downloadable CSV and Excel files in Gross domestic product: June 2025 quarter .

### 17 September 2025: Balance of payments and international investment position
Resolved 19 September 2025 We are experiencing some technical issues with Infoshare that may prevent the ratios table updating to include data for the June 2025 quarter. However, all data can be accessed via the CSV file under Download data on Balance of payments and international investment position: June 2025 quarter and via Infoshare Export Direct.

### 16 September 2025: Selected price indexes (SPI)
Resolved 19 September 2025 Three Food Price Index tables in Infoshare have not updated to include data for August 2025. However, the data can be accessed via the CSV file under Download data on Selected price indexes: August 2025 and via Infoshare using Export Direct.
The impacted tables are:
- Food Price Index for New Zealand (monthly)
- Food Price Index Level 2 Subgroups for New Zealand (monthly)
- Food Price Index Level 3 Classes for New Zealand (monthly).

### 9 September 2025: Business financial data (BDC), Manufacturing (MFG), Wholesale Trade (WTS)
We received updated information or made improvements to our methodology affecting the March 2024 to March 2025 quarters.
The changes are noted with a revision flag on the affected series in the published tables, CSV files, and relevant Infoshare series.
The impacted series are:
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,3)CC2CA – Textile, leather, clothing, and footwear manufacturing – sales, purchases, salaries and wages – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(1,2,3)CCCA
- BDCQ.SF(1,2)DD1CA – Electricity, gas, water, and waste services – sales, purchases – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(1,2)DDCA
- BDCQ.SF(1,2,3)EE1CA – Construction – sales, purchases, salaries and wages – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF(1,2,3)EECA
- BDCQ.SF1FF1CA – Wholesale trade – sales – and the corresponding higher-level industries – BDCQ.SF1FFCA
- MFGQ.SFD5(C,K)A – Beverage and tobacco product manufacturing – finished goods stocks – current prices and deflated – and the corresponding higher level industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)5(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFE(1,2,4,5)(C,K)A – Textiles, leather, clothing, and footwear manufacturing – sales, purchases, raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – current prices and deflated – and the corresponding higher level industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(1,2,4,5)(C,K)A
- MFGQ.SFL(4,5)(C,K)A – Transport equipment, machinery, and equipment manufacturing – raw materials stocks, finished goods stocks – current prices and deflated – and the corresponding higher level industries – MFGQ.SF(Y,Z)(4,5)(C,K)A
- WTSQ.SFF1CA – Commission-based wholesaling – sales – and the corresponding higher level industries – WTSQ.SFZ1CA
Subject category: Industry sectors

### 2 September 2025: Consumers price index (CPI)
We have updated historical figures for some quarters between the years 1914 and 1925. These periods were incorrectly loaded as 0; they have been updated to show as unavailable.
The table impacted is: CPI All Groups for New Zealand (Qrtly-Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec)
Series identifier: CPIQ.SE9A
Subject category: Economic indicators Group: Consumers Price Index – CPI

### 2 September 2025: Livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS)
We have revised the livestock slaughtering statistics (LSS) covering the period July 2024 to July 2025. The month revised was in June 2025.
LSS regions that were revised:
Regional Council by kill animal type – LSSM.SAC - Taranaki, Wanganui, Manawatu, Wellington
Total New Zealand by kill by animal type – LSSM.SAZ
Subject category: Industry sectors Group: Livestock Slaughtering – LSS
